example_usage:
Basic usage:

resource "cloudstack_network" "default" {
  name             = "test-network"
  cidr             = "10.0.0.0/16"
  network_offering = "Default Network"
  zone             = "zone-1"
}

argument_reference.name: display_text
argument_reference.description: The display text of the network.

argument_reference.name: cidr
argument_reference.description: The CIDR block for the network. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: gateway
argument_reference.description: Gateway that will be provided to the instances in this network. Defaults to the first usable IP in the range.

argument_reference.name: startip
argument_reference.description: Start of the IP block that will be available on the network. Defaults to the second available IP in the range.

argument_reference.name: endip
argument_reference.description: End of the IP block that will be available on the network. Defaults to the last available IP in the range.

argument_reference.name: network_offering
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the network offering to use for this network.

argument_reference.name: vlan
argument_reference.description: The VLAN number (1-4095) the network will use. This might be required by the Network Offering if specifyVlan=true is set. Only the ROOT admin can set this value.

argument_reference.name: vpc_id
argument_reference.description: The VPC ID in which to create this network. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: acl_id
argument_reference.description: The ACL ID that should be attached to the network or none if you do not want to attach an ACL. You can dynamically attach and swap ACL's, but if you want to detach an attached ACL and revert to using none, this will force a new resource to be created. (defaults none)

argument_reference.name: project
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the project to deploy this instance to. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: source_nat_ip
argument_reference.description: If set to true a public IP will be associated with the network. This is mainly used when the network supports the source NAT service which claims the first associated IP address. This prevents the ability to manage the IP address as an independent entity.

argument_reference.name: zone
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the zone where this network will be available. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

attributes_reference.name: display_text
attributes_reference.description: The display text of the network.

attributes_reference.name: source_nat_ip_id
attributes_reference.description: The ID of the associated source NAT IP.

import:
Networks can be imported; use <NETWORK ID> as the import ID. For example:

terraform import cloudstack_network.default 36619b20-5584-43bf-9a84-e242bacd5582When importing into a project you need to prefix the import ID with the project name:

terraform import cloudstack_network.default my-project/36619b20-5584-43bf-9a84-e242bacd5582
